permissions._comment: Repo-shared allowlist for the VM-side claude-vm-runner. Scoped to read-only diagnostic commands + the two narrow self-heal restart paths (ict-web-api.service via vm-web-api-recover.yml, ict-trader-live.service via operator-actions.yml::restart-bot-service). Per docs/claude/operator-actions.md and docs/claude/diag-relay.md, restarting ict-web-api has no trading blast radius (read-only API). Adding entries: mirror the audit pattern in scripts/ops/*.sh — narrow as possible.

hooks._comment: Project-level skill announcement. Forces every web-session to know /health-review exists and what its scope is, so we never repeat the 'Claude treats /health-review as a code-quality audit' confusion (operator feedback 2026-05-10). The hook simply prints a directive to stdout — Claude Code on the web injects stdout into the session-init context, so Claude reads it before the first user prompt. The cat fallback ('|| true') keeps the hook from failing the session if the skill file is ever missing.
hooks.command: echo '=== PROJECT-LEVEL SKILL: /health-review ==='; echo 'When the operator types /health-review or asks for "the health review" or "the layer-2 review", READ .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md and follow it exactly. Claude pulls the LIVE BOT runtime state itself via the diag relays (vm-diag-snapshot.yml / trainer-vm-diag.yml) — never ask the operator to paste a snapshot — and emits a JSON response per comms/schema/health_review_response.template.json. It is NOT a code review or codebase audit. Do NOT ask scoping questions like "both repos, full audit?" and do NOT fall back to the review or security-review skills.'; echo ''; echo 'Skill file:'; ls -la .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null || echo '(missing — should never happen on main)'

log="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/logs/notify_hook.log"; mkdir -p "$(dirname "$log")"; cp_line=$(grep -m1 '^## CP-' "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/claude/checkpoints/CHECKPOINT_LOG.md" 2>>"$log" || echo ''); [ -z "$cp_line" ] && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) skip: no CP entry found" >>"$log"; exit 0; }; cp_id=$(echo "$cp_line" | grep -oE 'CP-[0-9-]+' | head -1); title=$(echo "$cp_line" | sed 's/^## CP-[0-9-]* — //'); cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) firing $cp_id" >>"$log"; PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/notify_session.py session --checkpoint "$cp_id" --summary "$title" >>"$log" 2>&1; echo "$(date -Iseconds) exit=$?" >>"$log"; } || true
